title: acilepis peninsularis
intro: Acilepis peninsularis, also known as the Hill's lemongrass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's leaves and essential oil have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. Acilepis peninsularis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or essential oils, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
